title: Boston Marathon runner honors brother lost to suicide, supports fellow veterans
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Heather Diamond is running the Boston Marathon for Home Base after losing her brother to suicide.
Home Base, a partnership between Massachusetts General Hospital and The Red Sox Foundation, supports veterans' mental health.
Veterans face high suicide rates, with over 17 veteran suicides daily according to the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs.

title: Livestream: Day 3 of the Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial
author: John Waller
bullets: Prosecutors concluded their case by calling their final witness on Wednesday morning in the Kelsey Fitzsimmons trial.
NBC10 Boston is providing a livestream of the trial proceedings for Kelsey Fitzsimmons.
Kelsey Fitzsimmons opted for a bench trial, allowing a judge, not a jury, to determine her verdict.

title: Police release photo of ‘person of interest’ in Boston shooting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston police released a photo of a 'person of interest' linked to a Sunday evening shooting in the South End.
The suspect was seen on surveillance footage changing clothes and fleeing the scene near Washington and East Berkeley streets.
Police urge anyone with information to contact District D-4 detectives or submit anonymous tips through available channels.

title: Why did Kelsey Fitzsimmons choose to have a judge decide her fate?
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons opted for a bench trial for her assault case, avoiding a jury due to emotional complexities.
Her defense narrative involved mental health struggles, leading concerns that a jury might not favorably perceive mental illness defenses.
Judge Jeffrey T. Karp will decide her fate; Fitzsimmons faces up to five years in prison if convicted.

title: The road is only going to get harder for Bruins after ’embarrassing’ loss to Leafs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins lost 4-2 to Toronto Maple Leafs, missing key playoff race points.
Bruins allowed 12 odd-man rushes, criticized for poor special teams play.
Upcoming games include Buffalo Sabres and Minnesota Wild, crucial for playoff hopes.

title: Adam Schefter: No ‘under-the-table’ A.J. Brown trade in place for Patriots, Eagles
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: ESPN's Adam Schefter denies any under-the-table agreement between the Patriots and Eagles for trading A.J. Brown.
Philadelphia would face $43.5 million in dead money if Brown is traded before June 1, reducing to $16.353 million after.
The Patriots remain a potential destination for Brown, but no finalized trade can occur before the June 1 cap implications change.

title: This police officer is running Boston to support self-defense training
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Caroline Butts, a Milton police officer, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon supporting The Vanessa Marcotte Foundation.
The Vanessa Marcotte Foundation promotes violence prevention and self-defense, aiming to empower women and reduce harassment.
Butts aims to raise awareness and contribute to women's safety by combining her roles as a police officer and marathon runner.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (Mar. 25)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: A historic row-middle condo at 15 Marlborough St., Boston sold for $4.5 million, built in 1900.
In Arlington, a two-family multi-unit building at 39 Maynard St. sold for $1,475,000, built in 1926.
A one-family Cape Cod in Acton at 71 Liberty St., built in 1957, was sold for $515,000.

title: Man convicted more than 3 decades after raping 2 women at gunpoint in Framingham store
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Stephen Paul Gale, 73, was convicted for raping two women at gunpoint in a Framingham store in 1989.
Gale evaded capture for over three decades, living under multiple identities until identified through modern DNA technology.
Gale was arrested after a lengthy car chase in Los Angeles in 2024, and sentencing is set for March 30.

title: New Providence basketball coach goes viral for recruiting joke: ‘Just look at my wife’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bryan Hodgson introduced as new Providence men's basketball head coach, aiming to improve the team's recent sub-.500 performance.
Hodgson went viral for joking about his recruiting skills, referencing his wife Jordan as proof of his abilities.
Previously at South Florida, Hodgson led the Bulls to a 25-9 season and an NCAA tournament appearance in 2026.
